[2018-03-26 02:32] supergreyman (Greyman): I made a Reddit post which tries to describe the thought process of finding out what's a good strategy in any set. This is what I wrote:
```1) Determine econ size. Look for good absorber or rush units.

2) Determine which units are unviable. Some may be unviable based on econ size, or based on insufficient defensive support, or based on counters, etc. Click and drag unit panels to put unviable ones to the bottom.

3) List out possible strategies. Rearrange unit panels to visually group them for better readability. See which units are key attackers and key defensive units in each strategy.

4) Determine best strategy. I think in this phase, your understanding of how each unit works has a great impact. The more familiar you are with them, the better you'll become at evaluating best strategy.

5) See what kind of opening will help set-up your strategy. How many drones do you need to pump out key attackers and defenders per turn? For example, if you want to pump out Tantalum and Wall per turn, try to aim for 12 drones.```
I'm wondering if this is the type of thought procedure that top players go through at the beginning of each game. Sure, we're having more set-reading guides on sample sets (thanks to @307th), I wonder if it would be beneficial to have a general guideline on how to create initial game plan.
[2018-03-26 02:35] mrguy888: Is that list more helpful than just saying look at the units and play the right moves?
[2018-03-26 02:35] mrguy888: I would dare to say no.
[2018-03-26 02:37] mrguy888: "Determine best strategy" As a black box instruction to determining the best strategy is not going to help people.
[2018-03-26 02:37] darkeisbein: mrguy, the intuition guy
[2018-03-26 02:38] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I don't think there's a good way of determining whether X strategy is better than Y strategy unless you have a bunch of experience with those units.
[2018-03-26 02:40] supergreyman (Greyman): But when I read on set reading guides, I try to break down the thought process into step-by-step procedure. What to first look for, what to look for next, etc.
[2018-03-26 02:42] supergreyman (Greyman): And then when you see several seemingly good units in a set (like Tantalum Ray, Tesla, Frostbite, Hannibull, etc.), you would probably separate them into 2 different types of paths you could go for: blue/green and blue/red.
[2018-03-26 02:42] supergreyman (Greyman): So I'm wondering if this is the type of steps people go through, consciously or not.

[2018-03-26 03:09] redrame: that comes with a bit of practice, but in general I find advanced planning to sometimes be detrimental rather than helpful
[2018-03-26 03:10] redrame: prismata is a 2 player game
[2018-03-26 03:10] redrame: if you cover your opponent's side of the board and play the first 10 moves there are very few sets where you'll end up fine
[2018-03-26 03:11] redrame: specific big unit planning is pretty useful, but the main planning there is just to calculate out defense for the following turn and make sure you dont get breached while buying the unit

[2018-03-27 05:36] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): Rushing for big defenders is not the best choice, right? 
ive been playing against Master bot and i kept losing. Particularly, I've been rushing big absorbs like Zaetron, Omega Splitter and the uhhh 18RGGBB dude, but I somehow still lost because I can't build up damage afterwards.
[2018-03-27 05:38] .bky_1556 (bky): It depends on the defender.
[2018-03-27 05:38] f300xen (f0x): xaetron is just a really expensive matrix if you're rushing it, BBB units are not great to rush, yeah
[2018-03-27 05:38] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): also can someone explain Frontline to me please? I have to be wrong, but I felt that Wild Drone and Shredders are "bad" because they can be targetted
[2018-03-27 05:38] .bky_1556 (bky): Omega Splitter is okay for damage if you can keep buying them.
[2018-03-27 05:39] f300xen (f0x): wild drone is mostly for alternate openings
[2018-03-27 05:39] f300xen (f0x): shredders are good soak that create 1 threat
[2018-03-27 05:40] .bky_1556 (bky): Centurion, you typically get it out of a 3-engineer build, and you have the tech to get any efficient medium sized attackers. Combine that with the 2 attack and it's the best "rush" target of the three you mentioned.
[2018-03-27 05:41] redzonex (Redzone): centurion is also prompt which makes it easier to push out
[2018-03-27 05:41] .bky_1556 (bky): Xaetron, usually, you want to get a bit later. Note that heavy green non-breachproof does sometimes have trouble pushing for damage
[2018-03-27 05:41] redzonex (Redzone): and comes with attack so you can start putting pressure on your opponent earlier
[2018-03-27 05:42] .bky_1556 (bky): Omegas aren't prompt, so they're vulnerable to being "jammed" by an opponent buying burst to force you to buy a wall that turn.
[2018-03-27 05:42] f300xen (f0x): also they're vulnerable to losing because you bought 3 blastforges instead of something else
[2018-03-27 05:43] .bky_1556 (bky): But if your opponent goes big and you get the first omega out behind a wall, the omegas can carry themselves until you need to buy more walls to soak. Then the wheels come off the omega train but hopefully by then you've gotten enough value from the omegas to be ahead anyway.
[2018-03-27 05:44] .bky_1556 (bky): For that reason, I prefer synthesizer-conduit Omega to triple blastforge Omega.
[2018-03-27 05:44] .bky_1556 (bky): The synthesizer variant can arrange to have two floated green to forcefield to avoid making a wall.
[2018-03-27 05:46] .bky_1556 (bky): Wild drone has a couple of things you can do with it. You can buy it as part of an opening, netting +1 gold now but losing absorb value and income when it gets frontlined.
[2018-03-27 05:47] .bky_1556 (bky): If that +1 gold makes your opening more efficient, you'll still be ahead of your no-wild-drone baseline when it finally dies in 4-ish turns.
[2018-03-27 05:48] .bky_1556 (bky): You can also use it in a low econ red rush. The gold you save matters more...
[2018-03-27 05:49] amalloy: frontline units as "bad" because they can be attacked directly, but in exchange they're better than anything else you could buy for the price
[2018-03-27 05:49] .bky_1556 (bky): ...but this time you're happy when they click it because your soak and absorb are both more expensive than normal.
[2018-03-27 05:49] .bky_1556 (bky): So if it's convenient you can just throw one out and use it to buy defensive engineers or w/e.
[2018-03-27 05:49] .bky_1556 (bky): If they kill it they're not killing your prompt rhino.
[2018-03-27 05:51] .bky_1556 (bky): Shredders are different because they're cost-effective soak. You buy one when the opponent has 8+ damage so they can't exploit you.
[2018-03-27 05:51] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): so you actually dont click them in those situations
[2018-03-27 05:52] .bky_1556 (bky): Good for dealing with Scorchilla burst and the like.
[2018-03-27 05:52] .bky_1556 (bky): You are also *threatening* to click them.
[2018-03-27 05:53] .bky_1556 (bky): So they get a bit of bonus value from your opponent overdefending.
[2018-03-27 05:53] .bky_1556 (bky): Also, like Wild Drones, you can buy them when the opponent has a lot of attack
[2018-03-27 05:54] .bky_1556 (bky): and click them while full defending.
[2018-03-27 05:54] .bky_1556 (bky): If they frontline it, you need less defense next turn and, hey, you got a poke in.
[2018-03-27 05:54] .bky_1556 (bky): Finally, you can use shredders as a breachproof attacker.
[2018-03-27 05:55] .bky_1556 (bky): The frontline designation means nothing when they can breach for 4 anyway.
[2018-03-27 05:56] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): alright, thanks a lot buddy!
[2018-03-27 05:56] .bky_1556 (bky): Just be aware that mixing different sizes of frontline units makes them far more exploitable.
[2018-03-27 05:56] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): maybe ill actually beat the bot again this time :GWnanaPepoHype:
[2018-03-27 05:57] .bky_1556 (bky): Again, irrelevant in breachproof.

[2018-03-28 14:14] darkeisbein: Is it ever right to go mono green non bp (or rather only go bp once you can no longer defend?)
[2018-03-28 14:15] darkeisbein: I guess it might work out if you have some good green soak in the set + xaetron?
[2018-03-28 14:16] crash_overlord: Not saying this is the 100% correct strat
[2018-03-28 14:16] crash_overlord: or that either of us played amazingly
[2018-03-28 14:16] crash_overlord: but its a game I went monogreen in and I think is a set that you CAN go monogreen in if your opponent plays fast
[2018-03-28 14:17] darkeisbein: yeah I just played two games vs. mono green opponents and I won both times because I had wall absrob + soak the first game and feral warden soak the second game
[2018-03-28 14:18] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): Aegis+mahar+tantalum can easily be mono green
[2018-03-28 14:18] darkeisbein: I guess you really need mahar/Xae + some soak though right?
[2018-03-28 14:18] crash_overlord: Essentially you go monogreen if you have a green absorber and you don't have the econ to support other soak buildings (ie blue for only walls) or if theres an ever better soak in green.
[2018-03-28 14:18] darkeisbein: or just go hard for the bp strat
[2018-03-28 14:19] crash_overlord: Generally other techs will have efficient attackers though
[2018-03-28 14:19] crash_overlord: so going for those with your green absorber is generally correct
[2018-03-28 14:19] crash_overlord: but if you're getting animus just for tarsiers it might be worse than spamming gauss cannon since the animus will be worthless later in the game
[2018-03-28 14:21] darkeisbein: well I just won with animus for tarsiers 😄
[2018-03-28 14:21] darkeisbein: (and feral wardens for soak)
[2018-03-28 14:21] crash_overlord: Feral wardens are the big thing there
[2018-03-28 14:21] darkeisbein: I mean as soak they are worse walls
[2018-03-28 14:22] darkeisbein: still good soak in red I guess
[2018-03-28 14:22] p0lari (Polari): depends on how you value the resources
[2018-03-28 14:23] p0lari (Polari): on paper they are slightly worse than walls, but if you got red early for attackers and have no use for it now and can consider it free, it's better
[2018-03-28 14:23] crash_overlord: You both are forgetting that your opponent has to buy +1 defense
[2018-03-28 14:23] crash_overlord: because of the threat of breaching
[2018-03-28 14:23] crash_overlord: sure gambiting can happen
[2018-03-28 14:23] crash_overlord: but you can't really gambit a xae
[2018-03-28 14:24] darkeisbein: I can't credibly gambit a xae against a bp opponent 😄
[2018-03-28 14:24] p0lari (Polari): depends, if all enemy threat is real and you're absorbing on mahar (which is very likely in context) the feral threat is completely fake
[2018-03-28 14:24] p0lari (Polari): (though I have to admit I did forget about it)
[2018-03-28 14:26] darkeisbein: I guess red with a slightly worse wall is still scary since you have so much better splitters/gauss cannons
